retry_reason: Put operations release references; retrying would risk double-release. If pointer is NULL, it is a no-op; if pointer is non-NULL but refcount problematic, it indicates an invariant violation that retry cannot fix.
contract_summary: This is a cleanup helper that releases the BPF program and task references held in a bpf_task_work_ctx. It nullifies the pointers after release to prevent use-after-free. Callers must ensure that ctx is valid and that they are the last owner of these references (i.e., no other concurrent accesses). After calling this function, the ctx should not be used again without re-initialization. It safely handles NULL pointers (idempotent). It does not check for errors, as the put functions are expected to always succeed on valid objects. This function is intended for use in error paths or cleanup sequences.
